description.overview: Yorkies pack a lot of personality into a tiny package. They're feisty, bossy, and full of terrier spirit—don't let their small size fool you.
description.history: Developed in Yorkshire, England during the 1800s by Scottish weavers who brought terriers south. Originally used to catch rats in textile mills.
description.temperament: Yorkies are affectionate with their owners but can be suspicious of strangers. They have big personalities and don't know they're small. Can be barky.
description.health: Prone to dental issues, hypoglycemia, luxating patella, and collapsed trachea. Regular dental care and careful handling important.
description.grooming: High maintenance coat requiring daily brushing and regular professional grooming. Many owners opt for shorter 'puppy cuts.'
description.exercise: Low to moderate exercise needs. Short walks and indoor play. Good for apartments but still need daily activity.

not_for: Those wanting a rugged outdoor dog
verdict.summary: Yorkies are feisty, portable companions perfect for city living. They need regular grooming but don't shed much. Best for adults without young children who enjoy pampering a tiny but spirited companion.
